summary: Started M7-MAP-SITUATION-MODE-UX-001 after product-owner REQUEST_CHANGES rejected the current M7 content-lock UI/map experience. This task repairs the default player-facing map into a low-fidelity strategic Situation map before content lock can be reconsidered.

tests_run.result: Clean main synchronized with origin/main before task branch creation.

decisions.decision: Formal M7 content lock remains not accepted; UX recovery proceeds as scoped repair work rather than content-lock approval.
Start the map Situation-mode repair first because the product-owner feedback specifically identifies the rectangular grid map and unexplained lines as the leading blocker.

Repair must stay in client/read-model/map presentation boundaries and must not change simulation authority, protocol, save format, content-source data, manual node battle, server, telemetry, release, commercial, branding, or final art scope.

risks.summary: Map experience repair can drift into new authoritative geography, pathfinding, or campaign systems.
risks.mitigation: Task acceptance restricts implementation to existing read models and presentation-layer geometry/route semantics.

risks.summary: Route lines can remain visually noisy or semantically unclear if all links stay emphasized by default.
risks.mitigation: Situation mode must de-noise routes and provide localized legend semantics; full route network belongs in routes mode.

requested_action: Implement within allowed paths, produce a writer REVIEW handoff, and route to systems_architect. Do not open PR, merge, or mark CLOSED.
